'''Leroy Cudjoe''' (born 7 April 1988) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|SR}} for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. A product of the Huddersfield academy system, Leroy is a club legend, he was the captain of Huddersfield, and as well as representing [[England national rugby league team|England]], he has played his whole [[Super League]] career to date with [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], playing as a {{rlp|wg}}er, {{rlp|cn}}  and {{rlp|fb}} before moving into the pack, he has covered just about every position on the field for the Giants.

==Early life==
Cudjoe was born in [[Huddersfield]], [[West Yorkshire, England]] and is of [[Grenadian people|Grenadian]] descent, where his father's family originated.<ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/people/profiles/leroy-cudjoe-the-wings-speech-how-giant-came-to-terms-with-his-stammer-2307948.html| location=London| work=The Independent| date=7 July 2011| title=Leroy Cudjoe: The wing's speech: How Giant came to terms with his stammer}}</ref>

Leroy attended [[Newsome Academy|Newsome High School and Sports College]], and played junior rugby league for the [[Newsome Panthers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Huddersfield Giants and England World Cup star Leroy Cudjoe so proud to show off his Newsome Panthers colours |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-england-world-cup-6263697 |website=Yorkshire Live |access-date=8 April 2023 |date=2 November 2013}}</ref> He joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]] Academy at the age of 16.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/7151011/brown-cudjoe-sensational | title= Brown - Cudjoe 'sensational' | date=4 September 2011 | publisher=Sky Sports | accessdate=9 March 2020 }}</ref>

==Domestic career==
[[File:Leroy Cudjoe 2013 RLWC.jpg|thumb|right|Cudjoe playing for England]]
===Huddersfield Giants===
Cudjoe was awarded a full-time contract with the Giants at the end of the 2006 season.<ref>{{cite news |title=Rising star Leroy earns Super deal |url=https://www.thefreelibrary.com/Rising+star+Leroy+earns+Super+deal.-a0156200520 |access-date=8 April 2023 |work=Huddersfield Daily Examiner |date=21 December 2006}}</ref>  His 2007 season was partly disrupted due to a broken hand sustained in May, and he did not make a first team appearance although won the Huddersfield Reserves Player of the Year award at the end of the season.
He made his [[Super League]] début in the opening round of the 2008 season against [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Huddersfield Giants star Leroy Cudjoe sets testimonial targets |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-star-leroy-cudjoe-sets-testimonial-targets-1765377 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=12 September 2023 |date=3 January 2018}}</ref> He scored his first competitive try for the club in the 50–16 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in May 2008.
He continued to be a favourite during the 2011 and 2012 seasons at his hometown club [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

In the [[2013 Super League season]], he played 29 games as Huddersfield claimed the [[League Leaders Shield]] for the first time in 81 years.  Huddersfield would ultimately fall short of a grand final appearance that year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23850876|title=Huddersfield Giants 40-0 Wakefield|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

After 10 years with the club, Cudjoe was given a [[Testimonial match|testimonial]] in 2018 by Huddersfield.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Booth |first1=Mel |title=Giants captain Leroy Cudjoe awarded testimonial year in 2018 |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-captain-leroy-cudjoe-13537805 |website=YorkshireLive |access-date=26 January 2026 |date=28 August 2017}}</ref> A chronic knee problem limited his appearances in the 2018 and 2019 seasons.<ref>{{cite web |title=Luckless Huddersfield Giants stalwart Leroy Cudjoe is hoping his luck will change on restart |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/luckless-huddersfield-giants-stalwart-leroy-cudjoe-is-hoping-his-luck-will-change-on-restart-2504349 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=26 January 2026 |date=20 March 2020}}</ref>

In round 15 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he played his 300th match for Huddersfield and scored a try in the club's 40-26 victory over [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/huddersfield-vs-hull/55652/|title= Four tries from Jermaine McGillvary help Huddersfield Giants to 40-26 win over Hull FC|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref> On 28 May 2022, Cudjoe played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In September 2025, Cudjoe announced he would retire at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Giants legend Cudjoe to retire at end of season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn5q7x6xzpdo |website=BBC Sport |access-date=26 January 2026 |date=10 September 2025}}</ref>

==Representative career==
===Yorkshire===
He was selected to play for Yorkshire Academy in the fixture against Lancashire Academy in the 2005 season.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===England Academy===
This fixture is often seen as a trial game and a test for the players to get onto the England Academy team and although he initially made the England training squad, Leroy missed out on a place in the final England squad.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===England===
He played his first match for England in the [[2010 Four Nations]] tournament.

He played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}
